skill.name: maxhub-threads
skill.description: Threads data query assistant for post details, user profiles, search, comments, and reposts through the MaxHub API.
skill.summary: Query Threads social media data including posts, user profiles, search, comments, and reposts via the MaxHub API.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 87 potential issues across 6 files including hardcoded URLs, shell backtick execution, environment variable access, and weak crypto patterns. After AI evaluation, all findings are dismissed as false positives - they represent legitimate API documentation and standard curl-based API client patterns. The skill properly declares MAXHUB_API_KEY as sensitive, uses curl as documented in SKILL.md, and references the MaxHub API (aconfig.cn) which is the required external service. No prompt injection or malicious intent was detected.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to API endpoints in documentation
low_findings.description: 23 locations reference URLs for the MaxHub API (www.aconfig.cn) and example Threads URLs. FALSE POSITIVE: All URLs point to the legitimate API service used by this skill. These are documentation URLs showing users the API base URL, registration page, and example post URLs. No data exfiltration or malicious destinations detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs all point to aconfig.cn (API provider) or threads.com (example data source). No suspicious domains detected. Pattern matches expected behavior for a documented API client.

low_findings.title: Shell command execution in skill instructions
low_findings.description: 37 locations contain curl, npx, openclaw, and export commands in code blocks. FALSE POSITIVE: All commands are documentation examples shown in markdown code blocks or referenced as required binaries (curl). SKILL.md declares curl in requires.bins. These are legitimate commands the AI assistant uses to make API calls, not arbitrary execution vectors.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All commands are curl for API calls or setup commands in documentation. SKILL.md explicitly requires curl as a binary. No dynamic command injection or user-controlled arguments detected.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access for API authentication
low_findings.description: 15 locations reference MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable. FALSE POSITIVE: The skill properly declares MAXHUB_API_KEY as required:true and sensitive:true in SKILL.md metadata. The key is used only as a Bearer token for API authentication. The skill explicitly instructs the AI to keep API key values out of output.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: MAXHUB_API_KEY is a documented, required environment variable for legitimate API authentication. Properly declared as sensitive with user-facing setup instructions. No credential exfiltration patterns detected.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm flagged on API documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged Bearer token patterns and example post IDs as weak crypto algorithms. FALSE POSITIVE: No cryptographic operations are implemented. The flags match the 'Authorization: Bearer' header pattern (standard HTTP auth) and numeric example IDs in parameter documentation tables.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic algorithms are implemented in this skill. Flags are false matches on Bearer token header pattern and parameter table entries with example IDs.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance pattern in API documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged example post IDs and URLs as system reconnaissance. FALSE POSITIVE: These are legitimate example values in API parameter documentation tables, showing users what valid post IDs and URLs look like. No active probing or information gathering behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Example values are static documentation strings showing parameter formats. No dynamic system probing or reconnaissance behavior present.

low_findings.title: Combined risk pattern in API client skill
low_findings.description: Static analyzer heuristic flagged the combination of network + external_commands + env_access as dangerous. FALSE POSITIVE: This combination is the expected pattern for any legitimate API client skill. The skill connects to a documented API service, uses curl as its HTTP client, and accesses a declared API key. No obfuscation or data exfiltration detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network+commands+env_access triad is inherent to API client skills and is not suspicious here. The skill documentation is transparent about all three behaviors. No encoding, obfuscation, or hidden channels detected.

dangerous_patterns.title: Curl-based API calls with environment variable for authentication
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the AI to execute curl commands using MAXHUB_API_KEY as a Bearer token for API authentication. While legitimate for this skill type, the combination of external command execution (curl), environment variable access (API key), and network requests to external APIs represents elevated risk. Users should verify the API endpoint is trusted.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Pattern is standard for an API client but users should be aware that curl executes external commands and the API key is sent over the network. This is documented behavior, not a hidden threat.

content.user_title: Query Threads Social Media Data via API
content.value_statement: Manually checking Threads posts, user profiles, and trends across multiple tabs is slow and tedious. This skill lets you search, analyze, and compare Threads data through natural language queries without leaving your AI assistant.
actual_capabilities: Fetch Threads post details by post ID or URL
Search user profiles by keyword on Threads
Browse recent and top posts for any topic
Retrieve user posts, replies, and reposts
Support both English and Chinese language queries
Handle pagination for multi-page result sets
limitations: Requires a MaxHub API key from aconfig.cn to function
Data quality depends on the third-party API provider, not official Meta APIs
Cannot post, like, or interact with Threads content
API rate limits may restrict high-frequency queries

use_cases.title: Brand monitoring on Threads
use_cases.description: Marketing professionals can search for brand mentions, analyze sentiment around recent posts, and track engagement metrics across Threads conversations.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and brand strategists

use_cases.title: Content research and trend analysis
use_cases.description: Researchers and content creators can search for top posts on specific topics, compare engagement across creators, and identify trending discussions on Threads.

use_cases.title: Competitive intelligence gathering
use_cases.description: Product managers can monitor competitor activity on Threads by searching for company profiles, tracking their posts, and analyzing audience engagement patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Simple post lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the details of this Threads post: [paste post URL or ID]
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to quickly look up a single post's details including text, likes, and author info.

prompt_templates.title: Search for profiles on a topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for Threads profiles related to [topic/keyword] and show me their follower counts and bio information.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to find relevant accounts to follow or monitor on a specific topic.

prompt_templates.title: Compare user activity
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the recent posts from [user A] and [user B] and compare their engagement metrics side by side.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to compare activity and engagement between two Threads accounts.

prompt_templates.title: Deep content analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the recent posts from [username]. Summarize the topics they post about, their average engagement rate, and any trends in their posting schedule.
prompt_templates.target_user: A user wants a comprehensive analysis of an account's content strategy.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive account analysis for competitive or research purposes.

output_examples.input: Show me details of this Threads post: https://www.threads.com/@username/post/ABC123
output_examples.output:
Post by @username (1.2M followers)
Posted: 2 hours ago
Likes: 45.3K | Replies: 1.2K | Reposts: 892

"Exciting updates coming next week! Stay tuned for something we have been building for months."

Media: 1 image attached
Engagement rate: 3.8%

output_examples.input: Search for profiles related to artificial intelligence
output_examples.output:
Top profiles matching 'artificial intelligence':

1. @ai_research - 245K followers
   AI research lab. Daily papers and breakthroughs.

2. @tech_daily - 890K followers
   Tech news and AI coverage.

3. @ml_expert - 67K followers
   Machine learning tutorials and resources.

Found 12 profiles. Refine your search for more specific results.

best_practices: Configure your MAXHUB_API_KEY before making queries to avoid authentication errors
Use specific search terms and post IDs for more accurate and faster results
Start with browse mode for quick lookups, then use analyze mode for deeper insights
anti_patterns: Asking the assistant to modify or delete Threads content - this skill is read-only
Sharing your MAXHUB_API_KEY in conversation output or with others
Expecting real-time live data - API responses are cached and may have slight delays

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need a MaxHub API key from aconfig.cn. Register an account, create an API key in your dashboard, and set it as the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Can this skill post or reply on Threads?
faq.answer: No. This skill is read-only. It can only query Threads data like posts, profiles, comments, and reposts. It cannot create, edit, or delete content.

faq.question: What languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports both English and Chinese. The assistant will detect your language and respond in the same language with appropriate number formats.

faq.question: Is this an official Meta/Threads API tool?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses the MaxHub API (aconfig.cn), which is a third-party data provider. Data is for reference purposes and may have limitations compared to official APIs.

faq.question: How do I configure the API key on my system?
faq.answer: Run: export MAXHUB_API_KEY='your-key-here'. Or use OpenClaw: openclaw config set skills.entries.maxhub-threads.apiKey 'your-key'. Restart your assistant after configuration.

faq.question: What can I search for on Threads?
faq.answer: You can search for user profiles by keyword, look up post details by ID or URL, browse recent and top posts, and retrieve a user's posts, replies, and reposts.
